problem: Plan uses proposed_id, not dataset_id, and omits most primary-table fields.
required_change_before_implementation: Create full 55-column rows matching ibd_omics_datasets.csv header; rename proposed_id to dataset_id in any implementation-ready patch.

problem: Missing dataset_name, study_name, repository, repository_type, publication fields, disease, population, sample/tissue, omics_layer, platform, study_design, sample sizes, access fields, curation/status fields, usable flags, dataset_url, publication_url, notes, verification_notes, evidence_url.
required_change_before_implementation: Populate every registry column explicitly; use Unclear where public evidence is incomplete.

problem: IDs are currently available because max existing ID is DS108, but they should be reserved only if all six rows are implemented together.
required_change_before_implementation: Safe if appended sequentially; risky if partial implementation occurs. If only top candidates are added, assign contiguous IDs only to implemented rows.

problem: Plan splits one GEO SuperSeries/study into three rows and sets DS109 TRUE while DS110/DS111 FALSE. This is workable but requires DS109 to be clearly defined as the study-family anchor, not just one biopsy scRNA-seq assay.
required_change_before_implementation: Either add one multi-omics family row for GSE250498, or keep the three-row split but make DS109 name/notes explicitly say it is the counting anchor for the anti-integrin UC study family.

problem: Uses GSE250487 plus phs003502.v1.p1 but omits SuperSeries GSE250498 and PRJNA1054155 from accession/evidence.
required_change_before_implementation: Consider accession='GSE250498; GSE250487; phs003502.v1.p1; PRJNA1054155' or keep GSE250498 in notes/evidence if accession stays subseries-focused.

problem: Uses the same dbGaP accession as DS109/DS111; duplicate controlled raw companion may look like independent raw dataset if not explained.
required_change_before_implementation: Keep component_of_dataset_id=DS109 and overlap_notes should state phs003502.v1.p1 is a shared controlled raw companion, not a separate cohort.

problem: Same issue as DS110; CITE-seq is matched study-family component, not independent cohort.
required_change_before_implementation: Keep FALSE and component_of_dataset_id=DS109 if split is retained.

problem: TRUE may be okay, but public EGA evidence says 38 IBD patients, 80 stimulated samples, and dataset table lists 20 samples; publication link is unclear.
required_change_before_implementation: Keep TRUE only with notes that it is one controlled-access PBMC multiome resource, not 80 independent cohorts. Set publication_title/year/doi/pmid to Unclear unless sourced.

problem: Controlled-access EGA row needs explicit controlled_access=Yes, raw/processed availability conservative, and data_access_status controlled.
required_change_before_implementation: Use raw_data_available=No or Controlled/Unclear per local convention, processed_data_available=Unclear, controlled_access=Yes, data_access_status='Controlled access'.

problem: Unclear is appropriate. CosMx and Xenium are sibling platform components, likely same benchmark family, not clean parent/component rows.
required_change_before_implementation: Keep component_of_dataset_id blank unless a parent family row is created; do not set either TRUE until platform/sample overlap policy is decided.

problem: GSE312415 was not in the earlier top-candidate verification table, but public search supports it as the CosMx companion. Needs direct GEO/source verification before implementation.
required_change_before_implementation: Source-check GSE312415 directly and capture GEO title, sample count, raw files, BioProject, and publication linkage before adding.

problem: GSE312420 is verified as Xenium component, but GEO says citation missing while Nature 2026 article exists.
required_change_before_implementation: Add row only with conservative publication linkage notes unless GEO citation is updated or publication data availability directly confirms GSE312420.
